title: Adapt explains how it runs isolated computers for AI agents at scale
friendlyTitle: One AI agent, one safe computer
summary: Adapt described its infrastructure for giving each AI-agent conversation an isolated VM where the model can install tools, write code, browse, and call APIs. The post walks through its move from gVisor on GKE toward Firecracker-style microVM orchestration for faster, safer sandboxing.
friendlySummary: Adapt showed how it gives each AI agent its own protected computer to work in, instead of letting it run code directly on shared systems.
whyItMatters: As agents move from chat to action, sandbox design becomes the safety layer between useful automation and risky arbitrary code execution. Teams building agent products can learn from the trade-offs around isolation, startup speed, compatibility, and cost.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because helpful agents often need to run code. Safe sandboxes help them do useful work without risking the rest of the system.
